# Voice profile

**Authoritative rules live in `rules.md`.** This file is generated by `/voice:distill` from the corpus and supplements those rules with cadence, register, and grounding samples.

This is a starter template. Run `/voice:distill` after populating the corpus to regenerate it with patterns drawn from your actual writing.

## Default mode

(Two-sentence statement of the through-line: how long, how direct, how warm. /voice:distill will fill this in from the corpus.)

## Register matrix

| Register | When | Greeting | Sign-off |
|---|---|---|---|
| Close peer | Friends, repeat contacts | (your casual greeting) | (your casual sign-off) |
| Familiar warm | Service providers known by name | | |
| Default professional | Recruiters, new contacts | | |
| First-contact formal | Cold introductions | | |
| Transactional | Support tickets, cancellations | | |

## Phrase bank

To be filled in from corpus by `/voice:distill`. Group by register or function (openers, closers, affirmations, hedges, push-backs).

## Structural habits

To be filled in from corpus. Numbered list of patterns with one-sentence explanations and inline examples.

## Anti-voice

Patterns to avoid. The list in `rules.md` is canonical; this section adds register-specific ones.

## Vocabulary hot list

High-frequency words and phrases. Group by register.

## Verbatim grounding samples

Unedited samples per register, kept as blockquotes. These are what `/voice` pulls from when drafting.
